title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: - P1: [auto-push-after-commit.sh:27](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh:27) decides commit success from `stdout` substring checks only, then unconditionally runs `git push` on any tracked branch at [auto-push-after-commit.sh:35](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh:35). A rejected `git commit` usually fails via exit code/`stderr`, so this hook can publish the previous `HEAD` even when the new commit did not succeed. - P1: [recipe-loader.mjs:13](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/recipe-loader.mjs:13) hard-codes `~/.claude/rules/dev-recipes.json`, but the patch only adds [dev-recipes.json:1](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/rules/dev-recipes.json:1) under the repo and I could not find any sync/install change wiring that file into `~/.claude/rules`. On a fresh checkout, `--recipe`/auto-classification will silently fall back to default orchestration. - P2: [eval-skill.sh:332](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh:332) removed the old CJK/plaintext skip while scanning fenced shell blocks. Japanese explanatory lines inside valid bash examples will now be treated as binaries and counted as `PHANTOM_CMD`, causing false failures in skill evaluation. - P2: [context-monitor.sh:17](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh:17) doubles the checkpoint threshold and [context-monitor.sh:65](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh:65) downgrades the prior mandatory `/handover` interruption to a soft `/compact` suggestion. That weakens the existing context-loss safeguard and increases the chance of overrunning context before any checkpoint happens. No other syntax-level issues stood out; `node --check`/`bash -n` passed on the changed executable files.
